{"credits": [{"type": "finder", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13444627?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/codeswhite/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/codeswhite/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/codeswhite/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/codeswhite/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/codeswhite", "id": 13444627, "login": "codeswhite", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjEzNDQ0NjI3", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/codeswhite/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/codeswhite/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/codeswhite/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/codeswhite/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/codeswhite/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/codeswhite", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-48714", "cvss": {"score": 9.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 9.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-1321", "name": "Improperly Controlled Modification of Object Prototype Attributes ('Prototype Pollution')"}], "description": "### Impact

`i18next-http-middleware` ≤ 3.9.6's `missingKeyHandler` blocked the literal request-body keys `__proto__`, `constructor`, and `prototype` (added in 3.9.3, see GHSA-5fgg-jcpf-8jjw), but did not reject dotted variants such as `"__proto__.polluted"`. Downstream backends that split the missing-key string on a configured `keySeparator` (notably `i18next-fs-backend` ≤ 2.6.5) hand these keys to an unguarded `setPath()` walker that writes to `Object.prototype`.

Applications that expose `missingKeyHandler` to untrusted input **AND** use `i18next-fs-backend` ≤ 2.6.5 are directly exploitable for remote prototype pollution. Other downstream backends that split the missing-key string the same way may be similarly affected.

Depending on the host application, polluted prototype properties may cause crashes, corrupted translation behaviour, configuration poisoning, or bypasses of property-based security checks.

### Patches

Fixed in **i18next-http-middleware 3.9.7**. A new `utils.hasUnsafeKeySegment(key, keySeparator)` helper is now used by `missingKeyHandler`; the configured `i18next.options.keySeparator` is honoured (default `.`; `false` disables segment splitting and only the literal-key denylist applies). Legitimate dotted keys (e.g. `"header.title"`) are unaffected.

The root-cause fix has been shipped in `i18next-fs-backend` **2.6.6** — see the companion advisory.

### Workarounds

If users cannot upgrade immediately:

- Do not expose `missingKeyHandler` to untrusted users (mount it behind authentication, or remove the route).
- Add a request-body filter ahead of the handler that rejects any top-level key containing `__proto__`, `constructor`, or `prototype` after splitting on a configured `keySeparator`.
- Disable missing-key persistence (`saveMissing: false`) when accepting writes from untrusted input.

### Resources

- Original report by [@codeswhite](https://github.com/codeswhite).
- Companion advisory in `i18next-fs-backend`: [GHSA-2933-q333-qg83](https://github.com/i18next/i18next-fs-backend/security/advisories/GHSA-2933-q333-qg83).
- Previous `i18next-http-middleware` security release: GHSA-5fgg-jcpf-8jjw and GHSA-c3h8-g69v-pjrg (in 3.9.3).", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00419, "percentile": 0.3354}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-f49m-vf83-692w", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-25T17:28:12Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-f49m-vf83-692w", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-f49m-vf83-692w"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-48714"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-15T22:16:17Z", "published_at": "2026-06-25T17:28:12Z", "references": ["https://github.com/i18next/i18next-http-middleware/security/advisories/GHSA-f49m-vf83-692w", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-48714", "https://github.com/i18next/i18next-http-middleware/commit/7c6d26f137d3e940b8d229ca148bca38845faf49", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-f49m-vf83-692w"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/i18next/i18next-http-middleware/security-advisories/GHSA-f49m-vf83-692w", "severity": "critical", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/i18next/i18next-http-middleware", "summary": "i18next-http-middleware: MissingKeyHandler does not reject keys whose segments contain prototype-polluting names", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-25T17:28:12Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-f49m-vf83-692w", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "3.9.7", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "i18next-http-middleware"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 3.9.7"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: i18next-http-middleware: MissingKeyHandler does not reject keys whose segments contain prototype-polluting names
